steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.salus: 'Salus' is a noun meaning 'salvation' — it is the subject
hints.facta: 'Facta' is a perfect passive participle, forming a compound verb with 'est'

steps.prompt: What kind of verb form is 'facta est' together?
steps.reveal: facta est = has been made / has happened

hints.present passive indicative: Present passive would use the present stem, not a participle + 'est'
hints.pluperfect passive subjunctive: Pluperfect passive subjunctive would be 'facta esset'
hints.future perfect passive: Future perfect passive would be 'facta erit'

hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the principal parts of 'fero' (to carry/bear)
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the principal parts of 'fugio' (to flee)
hints.fallo, fallere, fefelli, falsum: These are the principal parts of 'fallo' (to deceive)

hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the principal parts of 'edo' (to eat) — the infinitive 'esse' is identical but the verb is different
hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the principal parts of 'eo' (to go)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are the principal parts of 'do' (to give)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'salutem' — 'salus' is the nominative subject
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'salutis' — 'salus' is nominative here
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'salute' — 'salus' is the nominative subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'salutis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'salutis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -us — 'salutis' ends in -is

hints.genitive: Genitive of 'domus' can be 'domus' or 'domi' — 'domui' is the dative singular
hints.ablative: Ablative of 'domus' is 'domo' or 'domu' — 'domui' is dative
hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'domus' — 'domui' is the dative singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'domus' has genitive in -us
hints.2nd declension: 'Domus' is irregular but primarily 4th declension with some 2nd declension forms
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'domus' has genitive in -us

hints.nominative: Nominative of 'hic' would be 'hic/haec/hoc' — 'huic' is dative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'hunc/hanc/hoc' — 'huic' is dative singular

hints.noun in ablative: 'Hodie' is indeclinable — it is an adverb, not a declined noun
hints.conjunction: A conjunction joins clauses — 'Hodie' modifies the time of the action
hints.preposition: A preposition governs a noun — 'Hodie' stands alone as an adverb

translation: Today salvation has come to this house
